perScholasAdvantage: Ask career services: "Which employers hired recent A+ graduates?"
Ask for introductions to TEKsystems, local MSPs, and help-desk recruiters.
Ask alumni: "Where did you get your first job after A+?"
Ask for mock interviews for technical support, not general customer service.
Ask whether alumni upskilling is available after the first role.

scripts.title: LinkedIn connection request
scripts.text: Hi [Name], I'm a CompTIA A+ certified Per Scholas graduate looking for my first IT support role in [remote / Houston]. I'd love to connect and learn from your experience in the field.

scripts.title: Informational interview request
scripts.text: Hi [Name], thanks for connecting! I'm early in my IT career — A+ certified through Per Scholas, with remote customer service experience — and I'm exploring entry-level help desk and technical support roles. Would you be open to a 15-minute call sometime in the next couple weeks to share how you got started or what you'd look for in a new hire? I'd really appreciate any advice.

scripts.title: Referral ask
scripts.text: Hi [Name], I saw you work at [Company from the zone list above] and I'm applying for entry-level IT support roles. I'm A+ certified through Per Scholas with remote customer service experience. Would you be willing to refer me for any open help desk or support roles, or point me to the right person on your team? Happy to send my resume.

scripts.title: Recruiter message
scripts.text: Hi [Name], I'm a Per Scholas graduate and CompTIA A+ certified. I also have remote customer service experience, so I'm comfortable supporting users by phone, chat, and email while documenting issues clearly. I'm looking for entry-level help desk, technical support, product support, or IT support roles around $20-$25/hr. Do you have any contract-to-hire or entry-level support roles where A+ and customer service would be a good fit?

scripts.title: Per Scholas alumni message
scripts.text: Hi [Name], I'm also connected to Per Scholas and recently earned A+. I'm looking for my first technical support/help desk role and have remote customer service experience. Would you be open to sharing where you found your first tech role or which employers were most open to new A+ grads?

scripts.title: Follow-up after applying
scripts.text: Hi [Name], I applied for the [Role] position and wanted to briefly introduce myself. I'm CompTIA A+ certified through Per Scholas and bring remote customer service experience, ticket documentation, and user-support skills. I'm especially interested in this role because it combines technical troubleshooting with customer support. Thank you for your consideration.
